description_long: This house features a rectangular, single-story structure with a dark, shingled gable roof and a stone foundation. The exterior walls are finished in white plaster, accented by wooden beams and trim. The front facade includes a central wooden door with visible weathering and a small covered porch supported by wooden posts. Windows are present on both the front and rear sides, with wooden shutters, some of which are open and others closed, and the glass appears to be missing or broken. Moss and plant growth are visible on parts of the walls and roof, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. A small brick chimney protrudes from the roof, adding to the rustic, utilitarian appearance.
description: A weathered, single-story house with a dark gable roof, stone foundation, wooden trim, and shuttered windows. Moss and plant growth accent the aged exterior, and a small porch shelters the main entry.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main door, a window with shutters, porch posts, and visible moss on the wall.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the gable end, a single window with shutters, and green moss on the wall and roof edge.
description_view_2: Rear view with two large windows, both with open shutters, and more extensive moss and plant growth.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with no door, a blank wall, and the chimney visible on the roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with shuttered windows on front and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door under a small porch with wooden posts and a simple overhang, weathered and mossy
